project.name: palace52
project.description: Palace52 is a full-stack Next.js memory-training web app for memorizing a deck of cards with memory palace and PAO techniques, with local auth, Prisma-backed training data, session history, profile features, and leaderboard foundations.

layers.name: Next.js App Routes
layers.description: Pages, layouts, route handlers, and public app entry points that compose the Palace52 application experience.

layers.name: Interactive UI Components
layers.description: Reusable React components and client-side experiences for building palaces, playing modes, charts, shells, and forms.

layers.name: Domain Training Logic
layers.description: Shared libraries for deck generation, play modes, scoring, dashboard metrics, leaderboard ranking, PAO presets, and formatting.

layers.name: Auth And Server Actions
layers.description: Authentication integration and server actions that mutate account, palace, PAO, review, and training-session data.

layers.name: Data And Prisma
layers.description: Prisma client access, database schema definitions, and SQL migrations for persisted Palace52 state.

layers.name: Project Config And Docs
layers.description: Repository documentation, TypeScript and Next.js configuration, package manifests, and local development metadata.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and package manifest to understand what Palace52 is, how it runs locally, and which framework stack it uses.

tour.title: Application Shell
tour.description: Follow the root layout into the public and authenticated shells to see how pages are wrapped, branded, and gated by login state.

tour.title: Authentication And Profile
tour.description: Inspect the local auth helpers, NextAuth route, account actions, and profile page that show user identity and saved training history.

tour.title: Build Palaces And PAO Decks
tour.description: Walk through the builder route, client builder component, and persistence actions for custom routes and PAO card mappings.

tour.title: Play Modes And Session Saving
tour.description: Study how the play page prepares a mode, runs the interactive game, grades answers, and saves completed session results.

tour.title: Dashboard And Leaderboard
tour.description: See how dashboard metrics, progress charts, and leaderboard rankings are calculated from completed training sessions.

tour.title: Database Model
tour.description: Finish at Prisma to connect the code paths back to persisted users, palaces, cards, sessions, reviews, and auth tables.
